title: About
text: Hello! I’m Manasvi, currently a Master’s student in Business Analytics at UC Irvine. I have a background in Computer Science and Economics, and I’m interested in how data and AI can be used to solve real-world business problems.

title: About
text:
My Background
I hold a B.A. in Computer Science and Economics from Whittier College, where I also served as captain of the women’s tennis team and developed strong leadership and teamwork skills. My academic and professional experience focuses on data analytics and business intelligence, applying data-driven insights to solve real-world business problems.

title: About
text:
What I Do
I focus on using data to support better decision-making and uncover insights that drive impact. My strengths include:

Working with data to identify patterns, build models, and generate insights that support business decisions.
Presenting complex information in a clear and actionable way using tools like Power BI, Tableau, and interactive dashboards.
Approaching problems with a structured mindset and translating data into practical business recommendations.

title: About
text:
My Interests
I’m particularly interested in the intersection of technology, healthcare, and business. I enjoy working on problems that have real-world impact and require both technical and strategic thinking.
Outside of work, I enjoy staying active, playing tennis, and exploring new ideas and creative projects.

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
Financial markets move fast, and a lot of investor sentiment now lives online on platforms like Reddit and Twitter.
But here’s the real question:
Do AI models actually understand financial language, or are they just picking up patterns?
In this project, we tested how different models interpret financial text, and more importantly, how they react when the meaning of a sentence changes.
We compared:

VADER (a simple rule-based model)
FinBERT (an AI model trained on financial text)
ChatGPT and Gemini (modern AI models)

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
Financial markets move fast, and a lot of investor sentiment now lives online on platforms like Reddit and Twitter.
But here’s the real question:
Do AI models actually understand financial language, or are they just picking up patterns?
In this project, we tested how different models interpret financial text, and more importantly, how they react when the meaning of a sentence changes.
We compared:

VADER (a simple rule-based model)
FinBERT (an AI model trained on financial text)
ChatGPT and Gemini (modern AI models)

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
What We Did
Instead of only checking accuracy, we went a step further.

We gave each model real financial posts from Reddit and Twitter

Asked them to classify sentiment as positive, negative, or neutral

Then we changed key words like “bullish” to “bearish”

And checked if the model’s prediction change when the meaning changed?

This helped us test whether the models were actually reasoning, not just guessing.

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
Key Findings

The simpler model, VADER, actually performed better than FinBERT on real-world social media data

FinBERT struggled and often predicted the same sentiment repeatedly

ChatGPT and Gemini performed the best overall, with more balanced and accurate predictions

But here’s the surprising part

None of the models were very good at understanding logical changes in meaning
Even when we flipped words like “buy” to “sell”, many models did not react properly.

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
What This Means
Most models can look accurate on paper but still fail basic reasoning.
This shows that:

Accuracy alone is not enough to judge AI performance

Models can miss important context in financial language

Testing how models handle changes in meaning is just as important

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
Tools Used

Python

HuggingFace (for FinBERT)

OpenAI API (ChatGPT)

Pandas and Scikit-learn

title: Do AI Models Actually Understand Financial Sentiment?
text:
What’s Next

Train models specifically on social media financial data

Explore combining multiple models for better performance

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Financial markets move fast, and critical information often lives in corporate announcements. This project builds a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) chatbot that allows users to ask financial questions and receive context-grounded answers with citations.
The system is designed for Saudi Stock Exchange (Tadawul) corporate announcements and supports multi-turn conversations, advanced retrieval, and a deployed web interface.
Live App:
Open Financial Chatbot

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Financial markets move fast, and critical information often lives in corporate announcements. This project builds a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) chatbot that allows users to ask financial questions and receive context-grounded answers with citations.
The system is designed for Saudi Stock Exchange (Tadawul) corporate announcements and supports multi-turn conversations, advanced retrieval, and a deployed web interface.
Live App:
Open Financial Chatbot

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Key Features

Citation-backed answers

Multi-turn conversation memory

Cross-encoder re-ranking for improved accuracy

Persistent vector database

Interactive Streamlit UI

Fully local execution

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
How It Works
The chatbot follows a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline:

User submits financial query

Relevant documents retrieved from vector database

Cross-encoder re-ranking improves accuracy

Language model generates response

Sources displayed with citations

This approach reduces hallucination and improves reliability for financial analysis. :contentReferenceoaicite:0

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Dataset

Domain: Corporate Financial Announcements

Documents: 1,800+

Content Includes:

Dividend announcements

Earnings reports

Corporate contracts

Regulatory disclosures


To improve retrieval quality: - Duplicate rows removed
- Missing summaries removed
- Smart chunking implemented

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Technical Architecture
Embedding Model
- sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
Vector Database
- ChromaDB (Persistent Mode)
Re-Ranking Model
- ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2
Generation Model
- google/flan-t5-base
This two-stage retrieval pipeline significantly improves answer accuracy. :contentReferenceoaicite:1

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Conversation Memory
The chatbot supports multi-turn conversations by storing recent interactions.
Example:
User:
“What dividend did Aramco announce?”
Follow-up:
“When was it announced?”
The system maintains context to improve retrieval accuracy.

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Deployment
The chatbot is deployed using Streamlit with:

Chat-style interface

Expandable citations

Input validation

Public deployment support

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Tools Used

Python

ChromaDB

Sentence Transformers

Hugging Face Transformers

Streamlit

Pandas

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
GitHub Repository
View full project and code:
Code Repository

title: RAG-Powered Financial Announcement Chatbot
text:
Impact
This project demonstrates how RAG systems can improve reliability in financial NLP applications by combining retrieval, re-ranking, and generation into a single pipeline.
It highlights practical applications of:

Large Language Models

Information Retrieval

NLP

Financial Analytics
